items.mistake: Computing distance without scaling features of different units
items.fix: Standardize or normalize features (e.g. z-score or min-max per column) before L2 or L1 distance.
items.why: A feature measured in thousands can dominate the norm even when it is less informative than a 0–1 feature.
items.example: Height in cm (170) and income in thousands (80) make L2 distance almost entirely income-driven; after z-scoring both columns, neighbors reflect balanced similarity.

items.mistake: Using Euclidean distance on high-dimensional sparse text counts
items.fix: Prefer cosine similarity or L1 on appropriately weighted sparse vectors, or use embeddings.
items.why: Sparse high-dimensional vectors make L2 distances less meaningful and expensive; angle often matters more than magnitude.
items.example: Two bag-of-words vectors with different document lengths can be far in L2 but nearly identical in direction; cosine similarity returns ~1.0.

items.mistake: Confusing squared L2 distance with L2 distance in implementations
items.fix: Check whether a library returns ||u-v|| or ||u-v||²; use `squared=False` explicitly when needed.
items.why: k-NN tie-breaking, thresholding and gradient formulas change when distances are squared.
items.example: sklearn `pairwise_distances` defaults to Euclidean (not squared); some custom losses use squared error without taking the square root.
